strategy.name: Event-driven momentum scalper
strategy.plainEnglish: Fade weakest major on relative-weakness reads. Hard stops, tight R:R.

openPositions.reason: Oracle's live read: ETH is the weakest major, ask-heavy book. If SOL leads risk-on, ETH still lags. If macro rolls (which it just did on the SOL flush), ETH falls faster. Clean relative-weakness short.

rootCauseReview.plainEnglishSummary: The loss got big because the trade stayed open after the original bounce idea was already broken.
rootCauseReview.whatWentWrong: There was no fast invalidation discipline. The position got treated like it deserved time when it should have been cut earlier.
rootCauseReview.nextTime: If the bounce thesis fails and the market starts trending the other way, exit fast instead of hoping it snaps back.

closedTrades.reason: BTC grinding lower all night, broke 74400, no bounce. Overnight dump continuation short.

rootCauseReview.plainEnglishSummary: BTC reversed the overnight dump and broke back above 74600 instead of continuing down. The continuation thesis failed on the morning bid.
rootCauseReview.whatWentWrong: Short continuation entry did not anticipate the overnight dump getting retraced. No invalidation planned other than the hard stop. Position got left unmanaged during session transition.
rootCauseReview.nextTime: For overnight continuation trades, define a time-based stop (e.g., if BTC holds above X for Y minutes, exit before the hard stop). Don't leave unmanaged shorts through session transitions.

hourlyMarks.note: Closed ETH -144.16. Opened BTC SHORT 25x from 74295.5
Closed BTC SHORT -36.44 (stopped at 74600). Opened ETH SHORT 10x from 2340 (Oracle relative-weakness read).

notes: Session handoff 2026-04-16 20:20 UTC: reconciled stale BTC SHORT (stopped out during session transition), opened ETH SHORT on Oracle's relative-weakness read. Down -180.60 total, need to earn it back.
